messages.content:
summarize the following:

US factory activity contracted for an eighth month in June, slipping to the weakest level in more than three years as production, employment and input prices retreated.
The Institute for Supply Management’s manufacturing gauge fell to 46, the weakest since May 2020, from 46.9 a month earlier, according to data released Monday. The current stretch of readings below 50, which indicates shrinking activity, is the longest since 2008-2009. 
The reading was also worse than all but one estimate in a Bloomberg survey of economists.


The decline in the ISM production gauge, which also fell to the lowest level since May 2020, suggests demand for merchandise remains weak. The index of new orders contracted for a 10th straight month and order backlogs shrank, which may help explain a pullback in a measure of manufacturing employment.
The ISM gauge retreated to a three-month low and, at 48.1, indicates fewer producers adding to payrolls.
Many Americans continue to limit their spending on merchandise as they rotate to services and experiences. Others are simply tightening their belts as still-high inflation takes a toll on their incomes.
Eleven industries reported shrinking activity in June, led by plastics and rubber products, wood products and textile mills.
Select ISM Industry Comments
“Customer orders have definitely slowed down. Our company thought the second half of 2023 would be better than the first half, but this doesn’t seem to be the case.” — Chemical Products
“Maintaining a strong order backlog. Continue to struggle with hiring hourly factory workers and finding qualified management candidates — higher turnover than desired. Pricing has stabilized, but labor costs remain high.” — Primary Metals
“The slowing US economy is causing the business forecast to be revised/reduced for the remainder of 2023. Customers are less inclined to purchase far in advance.” — Computer and Electronic Products
“Orders and business are steady with a healthy backlog, but new prospective orders seem to be getting pushed back into 2024.” — Machinery
At the same time, recent government data has shown business demand for equipment is still healthy and, if sustained, could provide some support for manufacturers.
“Demand remains weak, production is slowing due to lack of work, and suppliers have capacity,” Timothy Fiore, chair of the ISM Manufacturing Business Survey Committee, said in a statement. “Panelists’ companies reduced production and began using layoffs to manage head counts, to a greater extent than in prior months, amid mixed sentiment about when significant growth will return.”
Shrinking Inventory
The ISM gauge of customer stockpiles shrank at the fastest pace since October, while the index of factory inventories dropped to the lowest level since 2014.
Producers are also finding relief in declining commodities prices. The group’s index of prices paid for materials slid to 41.8, the lowest this year.

US factory activity contracted for the eighth consecutive month in June, reaching its weakest level in over three years. The Institute for Supply Management's manufacturing gauge fell to 46, indicating shrinking activity. This decline was worse than all but one estimate in a Bloomberg survey of economists. The decrease in the production gauge suggests weak demand, with new orders contracting for a tenth straight month. Order backlogs also shrank, potentially contributing to a decline in manufacturing employment. Eleven industries, including plastics, rubber products, wood products, and textile mills, reported shrinking activity. The decline in factory inventories and declining prices for materials provide some relief for producers. However, the weak activity and uncertainty surrounding future growth are leading some companies to reduce production and implement layoffs.

summarize the following:

A key segment of the US Treasury yield curve approached its most inverted level in decades Monday as traders priced in further Federal Reserve policy tightening.

The two-year note’s yield exceeded the 10-year rate by as much as 110.8 basis points as the shorter-maturity rate reached 4.96%. The inversion touched 110.9 basis points in March, a level last seen in the early 1980s, according to data compiled by Bloomberg.

The inversion eased to around 108.5 basis points after the ISM manufacturing gauge for June unexpectedly declined to a three-year low. In a shortened trading session ahead of a US holiday, Treasury two-year yields dropped to as low as 4.84% before climbing to nearly 4.94%. The 10-year yield rose to about 3.85%. 
“The near-term risks are that more rate hikes must be priced, that the Treasury curve flattens more, and that the pace of slowing both within the inflation and employment data does not satisfy policymakers and the markets,” John Brady, manging director at RJ O’Brien, wrote to clients.


The closely watched barometer of recession risk has ranged from inverted by as much as 111 basis points to as little as 27 basis points since March.

The flattening trend has gathered pace as expectations for the Fed’s path move toward pricing in a quarter-point interest-rate hike this month and another increase by year-end. Swap contracts currently price in about 22 basis points of tightening for the July 26 decision. They fully reflect an increase by September and about half of an additional boost by year-end.

Interest-rate strategists at Bank of America said in a report released Monday that positioning metrics suggest the curve “is prone to flatten further.”

The central bank left rates unchanged last month following 10 straight rate increases to assess how elevated policy rates are impacting the economy. But stronger-than-expected economic data released since then suggest additional tightening is likely.
The first major economic indicators for June are being released this week, culminating with employment data on Friday. US markets will be closed Tuesday for US Independence Day.

The yield curve of US Treasury bonds is approaching its most inverted level in decades, indicating expectations of further Federal Reserve policy tightening. The yield on the two-year note exceeded the 10-year rate by as much as 110.8 basis points, reaching a level not seen since the early 1980s. The inversion eased slightly after the ISM manufacturing gauge for June unexpectedly declined. The trend of a flattening yield curve has accelerated as the market anticipates interest rate hikes by the Federal Reserve. Swap contracts currently price in tightening for the July decision and reflect the possibility of another increase by year-end. Interest-rate strategists suggest that the curve is likely to flatten further. Strong economic data released recently suggests additional tightening is probable.

modify "The yield curve of US Treasury bonds is approaching its most inverted level in decades, indicating expectations of further Federal Reserve policy tightening. The yield on the two-year note exceeded the 10-year rate by as much as 110.8 basis points, reaching a level not seen since the early 1980s. The inversion eased slightly after the ISM manufacturing gauge for June unexpectedly declined. The trend of a flattening yield curve has accelerated as the market anticipates interest rate hikes by the Federal Reserve. As strong economic data released recently suggests additional tightening, interest-rate strategists suggest that the curve is likely to flatten further. "

The yield curve for US Treasury bonds is becoming increasingly inverted, signaling expectations of tighter Federal Reserve policies. The yield on the two-year note has surpassed the 10-year rate by up to 110.8 basis points, reaching a level not witnessed since the early 1980s. However, this inversion slightly eased following a decline in the ISM manufacturing gauge for June. The flattening of the yield curve has gained momentum due to market expectations of interest rate hikes by the Federal Reserve. With recent strong economic data supporting the notion of further tightening, interest-rate strategists predict that the curve will continue to flatten.

give a title to the following:

US factory activity contracted for the eighth consecutive month in June, reaching its weakest level in over three years. According to data released on Monday, the ISM manufacturing index slumped to 46 in June from 46.9 in May, indicating the longest shrinking activity since 2008-2009. The decrease in the production gauge suggests weak demand, with new orders contracting for a tenth straight month.  

The yield curve of US Treasury bonds is approaching its most inverted level in decades, signaling expectations of further Federal Reserve policy tightening. The yield on the two-year note exceeded the 10-year rate by up to 110.8 basis points, reaching a level not seen since the early 1980s. The inversion eased slightly after the ISM manufacturing gauge for June unexpectedly declined. The trend of a flattening yield curve has accelerated as the market anticipates interest rate hikes by the Federal Reserve.
Title: US Factory Activity Contracts for Eighth Month as Yield Curve Approaches Decades-Long Inversion
give an alternative title
US Factory Activity Contracts for Eighth Straight Month, Yield Curve Signals Expectations of Further Fed Tightening

translate the following in english:

根据国务院部署安排，近期国家发展改革委建立与民营企业沟通交流机制。7月3日，郑栅洁主任主持召开民营企业家座谈会，与三一集团、奥克斯集团、圆通速递、波司登、农夫山泉等5家民营企业负责人进行了深入讨论交流，认真听取民营企业经营发展的真实情况、面临的困难问题和相关政策建议。

　　5位民营企业家结合企业发展实际，分析了国内外环境变化对企业发展带来的机遇和挑战，介绍了生产经营过程中遇到的一些实际困难，并对进一步优化民营企业发展的法律和制度环境提出了有针对性的意见建议。

　　郑栅洁表示，国家发展改革委将持续发挥民营企业沟通交流机制作用，不断认真倾听民营企业家的真实想法，研究提出务实管用的政策举措，尽力帮助企业解决实际困难，为民营企业营造良好的发展环境。

According to the deployment and arrangement of the State Council, the National Development and Reform Commission has recently established a communication mechanism with private enterprises. On July 3rd, Director Zheng Zhongjie presided over a symposium with the leaders of five private enterprises, including Sany Group, Aux Group, YTO Express, Bosideng, and Nongfu Spring. They had in-depth discussions and exchanges, attentively listened to the real situation and difficulties faced by private enterprises in their business development, and solicited relevant policy suggestions.

The five private entrepreneurs analyzed the opportunities and challenges brought by changes in the domestic and international environment to the development of enterprises, shared some practical difficulties encountered in their production and operation processes, and put forward targeted opinions and suggestions for further optimizing the legal and institutional environment for the development of private enterprises.

Zheng Zhongjie stated that the National Development and Reform Commission will continue to play a role in the communication mechanism with private enterprises, attentively listen to the real thoughts of private entrepreneurs, study and propose practical and effective policy measures, and do their best to help enterprises solve practical difficulties and create a favorable development environment for private enterprises.

modify "On July 3rd, Zheng Shanjie, head of China’s top economic planning agency NDRC, held a forum with the leaders of five private enterprises, including Sany Group, Aux Group, YTO Express, Bosideng, and Nongfu Spring. The five private companies shared about their opportunities and challenges amid changes in the domestic and international business environment, and gave suggestions for further optimizing the legal and institutional environment for the private sector. Although no detail of the planned policies is published, Zheng promised to keep listening to private businessmen and create sound conditions for development of the private sectors."

On July 3rd, Zheng Shanjie, the Director of China's National Development and Reform Commission (NDRC), organized a forum with the leaders of five private enterprises, namely Sany Group, Aux Group, YTO Express, Bosideng, and Nongfu Spring. During the forum, the representatives of these companies discussed the opportunities and challenges they are facing amid changes in the domestic and international business environment. They also provided valuable suggestions for further improving the legal and institutional framework for the private sector. While specific details about forthcoming policies were not disclosed, Zheng assured the private business leaders that their voices would continue to be heard and emphasized the commitment to foster a supportive environment for the growth of the private sector.

give a title to "On July 3rd, Zheng Shanjie, head of China’s National Development and Reform Commission (NDRC), organized a forum with the leaders of five private enterprises, including Sany Group, Aux Group, YTO Express, Bosideng, and Nongfu Spring. During the forum, the representatives of these companies discussed the opportunities and challenges they are facing amid changes in the domestic and international business environment. They also provided suggestions for further improving the legal and institutional framework for the private sector. While specific details about forthcoming policies were not disclosed, Zheng vowed to keep listening to private businessmen and emphasized the commitment to foster a supportive environment for the growth of the private sector."
Title: NDRC Director Holds Forum with Private Enterprise Leaders, Emphasizes Support for Private Sector Growth
give an alternative title
Title: NDRC Engages with Private Enterprises to Enhance Business Environment and Support Growth

translate the following in english:

比亞迪 （01211） 近期進軍全球多地，據外媒報道，比亞迪的正規銷售店「BYD AUTO福岡西」於7月1日在日本福岡市西區的商業設施「Marinoa City FUKUOKA」開業，並是首次在日本九州地區開店。

新店展示了紅色和灰色的兩輛5座SUV「ATTO 3」。報道指，公司正在考慮把展示車輛換成預定今後在日本國內上市的小型掀背車「海豚（DOLPHIN）」和4門轎車「海豹（SEAL）」。另外，新店除了可以試乘試駕之外，還提供點檢、車檢和維修服務，展廳的面積約為147平方米。

比亞迪於今年2月在橫濱市開設了日本國內首家門市，迄今共開設了6家，公司計劃到2025年底增加到100家。位於橫濱市的比亞迪BYD Auto Japan社長東福寺厚樹表示，將根據人口和商圈的大小，將在福岡縣內增加到4家店。

Recently, BYD (01211) has been expanding its presence in multiple locations worldwide. According to foreign media reports, BYD's authorized dealership, "BYD AUTO Fukuoka West," opened on July 1st in the Marinoa City Fukuoka commercial facility in the Nishi ward of Fukuoka City, Japan. This is the first store to open in the Kyushu region of Japan.

The new store showcased two 5-seat SUVs, the "ATTO 3," in red and gray. The report suggests that the company is considering replacing the display vehicles with the upcoming small hatchback car, "Dolphin," and the 4-door sedan, "Seal," which are planned for domestic release in Japan. In addition to test drives, the new store also offers inspections, vehicle checks, and maintenance services, with a showroom area of approximately 147 square meters.

BYD opened its first domestic store in Yokohama City in February of this year and has since opened a total of six stores. The company plans to increase the number of stores to 100 by the end of 2025. Atsuki Tofukuji, President of BYD Auto Japan located in Yokohama City, stated that, based on the size of the population and business districts, they aim to have four stores in Fukuoka Prefecture.
